title: Add coupon discount support to charge endpoint
summary: This PR introduces coupon discount support on POST /charge. There are several blocking issues: a raw `console.log` of the request body (including full card number and CVC) that bypasses the project's pino redaction layer, and a logic bug that makes `discount_amount` always return 0. Beyond the blockers, the catch block that silently swallows discount errors, the ability for fixed-amount coupons to produce negative charges, missing schema validation for `couponCode`, camelCase/snake_case inconsistency in the response, heavy use of `any` typing, and the complete absence of tests all need to be addressed before this can merge.

findings.title: Raw card data logged via console.log, bypassing pino redaction
findings.message: Line 16 calls `console.log('DEBUG charge body:', JSON.stringify(theData))` on the unsanitised request body before Zod validation runs. The project already has a pino logger configured in `src/logger.ts` with explicit `redact` paths for `card.number` and `card.cvc`. `console.log` bypasses that entirely, so full PANs and CVCs land in plaintext in every log sink (stdout, log aggregators, etc.). This is a PCI DSS violation. Remove the debug log entirely; if request tracing is needed, use `logger.debug({ requestId: req.id }, 'charge_request')` — the pino config will automatically censor the card fields.

findings.title: discount_amount is always 0 — original amount overwritten before subtraction
findings.message: Line 23 writes `payload.amount = finalAmount` (the discounted value). Line 35 then computes `response.discount_amount = payload.amount - finalAmount`, which is `finalAmount - finalAmount = 0` every time. The core user-facing feature — showing the saving — is completely broken. Fix: capture the original amount before the try block, e.g. `const originalAmount = payload.amount;`, then compute `response.discount_amount = originalAmount - finalAmount`.

findings.title: Coupon errors silently swallowed — customer charged full price with no signal
findings.message: The inner try/catch around `doStuff` has an empty `catch(e) { // TODO }` body. Any future exception from the discount path (e.g. a code-path bug in a new strategy) will be eaten silently: no log, no metric, no error response. The customer is charged full price with no indication that the promised discount wasn't applied. At minimum this must log the error via `logger.error`. Depending on product requirements, it may need to abort the charge with a 422 rather than charge the wrong amount.

findings.title: FixedCouponStrategy.apply can return a negative amount
findings.message: `return amount - this.amt` has no floor. Applying TENOFF (1000 cents) to a 300-cent charge yields -700. This propagates as the `finalAmount` into `PaymentsService.charge()`, where `assertPositiveAmount(-700)` throws a plain `Error`. That error is not an `AppError`, so the middleware in `errorHandler.ts` returns a 500 instead of a meaningful 4xx. Fix: `return Math.max(0, amount - this.amt)`, and optionally throw a `validationError` at the controller level if the discounted amount would be zero or below.

findings.title: couponCode is not in chargeSchema — read from raw unvalidated body
findings.message: `couponCode` is extracted directly from `req.body` before Zod runs (`theData.couponCode`), so it receives no length limit, no character-set constraint, and no type check. An attacker can send a 10 MB string or inject special characters. Add `couponCode: z.string().regex(/^[A-Z0-9]{1,20}$/).optional()` to `chargeSchema` and read it from the validated `payload` instead of the raw body.

findings.title: Response fields use snake_case; all existing Transaction fields use camelCase
findings.message: The `Transaction` type (`domain/transaction.ts`) uses camelCase throughout: `cardLast4`, `customerId`, `gatewayReference`, `createdAt`. The new fields `discount_amount` and `coupon_code` break this convention. Rename to `discountAmount` and `couponCode` to match. Also, the `response: any` cast should be replaced with a properly typed interface (e.g. `ChargeResponse extends Transaction`) so the compiler enforces the contract going forward.

findings.title: console.log used instead of project logger (pino)
findings.message: Line 60 uses `console.log('DEBUG: unknown coupon code:', couponCode)`. Every other module in the codebase (e.g. `paymentsService.ts`, `errorHandler.ts`) uses the shared pino logger from `src/logger.ts`. Replace with `logger.warn({ couponCode }, 'unknown_coupon_code')`. Note: an unknown coupon submitted by an end-user is also worth considering from a security angle — structured logging makes it easy to alert on coupon-enumeration patterns.

findings.title: Public exported function named doStuff with several poor variable names
findings.message: `doStuff` is exported as part of the module's public API and called from the controller. It should be named `applyCouponDiscount` or similar. Inside the file: `tmp`, `result2`, `x`, `p`, and `amt` all obscure intent. `var` is used on lines 16 and 58 where the rest of the codebase (and TypeScript best practice) uses `const`/`let`. `==` loose equality is used throughout `CouponHandlerFactory.build` where `===` should be used.

findings.title: Zero test coverage for the new coupon functionality
findings.message: The PR test plan explicitly marks unit tests as a TODO. No test exercises: (1) a valid coupon reducing the charged amount, (2) an invalid/unknown coupon being ignored, (3) the `discountAmount` field in the response, (4) a fixed coupon applied to an amount smaller than the discount, or (5) no coupon supplied. Given the logic bug found in `discount_amount` (always 0), these tests would have caught the issue immediately. They should be in this PR, not a follow-up.
